service.description: Azure AI Foundry appears across 13 normalized findings in 1 checklist families. 1 lower-confidence families broaden design coverage.
service.whatThisMeans: This service is covered mainly by preview or mixed-confidence guidance. Use it for specialist review with explicit validation.

notes: Availability is mapped to the umbrella Microsoft Foundry offering because the source feed groups several Foundry capabilities together.

quality.label: Preview · Medium confidence
quality.summary: Use with architectural judgment. Preview content is valuable, but it should not be treated as equivalent to mature review baselines.

items.text: Configure the AI managed network and use private endpoints.
Implement a monitoring system to ensure that AI workloads remain aligned with KPIs.
Limit outbound traffic from your AI resources.
Proactively identify performance bottlenecks and anomalies.
Schedule regular retraining based on model performance or business needs to ensure the AI system stays relevant.
Use distinct workspaces to organize and manage AI artifacts like datasets, models, and experiments.
Disable inferencing via Azure AI Foundry to prevent API Gateway bypass.
Establish model promotion process to promote trained, fine-tuned, and retrained models to higher environments based on performance criteria.
Implement multi-region deployments to ensure high availability and resiliency for Azure AI Foundry.
Manage model versioning and detect drift and set alerts when model predictions or LLM responses start to deviate from expected behavior.
Standardize compute management for Azure AI Foundry.
Test the effectiveness of grounding by using tools like prompt flow.
Define and enforce a policy to automatically shutdown Azure AI Foundry and Azure Machine Learning compute instances.

notes: Availability is mapped to the umbrella Microsoft Foundry offering because the source feed groups several Foundry capabilities together.
